title: BN5101 Virtual laboratory Activity

questions.prompt: Flow cytometry results percentage of cells

free_text_rubric: Q1 Part A [3 pts]: 1 pt for observing Mutant RNAs 1 & 2 have one peak (~>100 units) compared to two in control. 1 pt for observing Mutant RNA 3 shows <2C and >4C content. 1 pt for noting Mutant RNA 3 has 2 peaks corresponding to 2C/4C but different distribution.
Q1 Part B [3 pts]: 1 pt each for identifying G2/M arrest for #1 & #2, and abnormal chromosome numbers for #3.
Q1 Part C [1 pt]: Cells in G2 and M have identical DNA content.
Q2 Part A [2 pts]: 1 pt for internal control, 1 pt for ensuring equal sample loading.
Q2 Part B [3 pts]: Each correctly identified as G2 or M (1 pt each).
Q2 Part C [3 pts]: 1 pt explaining #1 & #2 arrest based on high Cyclin B, no Cyclin E, and uncleaved cohesin. 1 pt explaining #3 based on heterogeneous population consistent with WB/Flow data.
Q3 Part A [12 pts]: Accuracy of observations for chromosomes and spindles compared to control for #1-3 (4 pts each).
Q4 Part A [15 pts]: Rationale for hypotheses covering Flow, WB, and Microscopy analyses (5 pts per condition).
Q4 Part B [12 pts]: Target proposals and justification for #1 (Separase/APC), #2 (Spindle attachment protein), and #3 (Mad2/Securin) (4 pts each).
